title: The Punctured Brillouin Zone Boundary Sum Vanishes
summary: Excise small balls around all bad points, integrate the exterior derivative of the pulled-back closed form over the punctured Brillouin zone, and use Stokes's theorem to obtain the vanishing total charge.
assumptions: The set of bad points is finite and isolated.
regime: Global topological analysis on the punctured Brillouin zone.
scope: Core proof fragment in the degree-theoretic derivation of Nielsen-Ninomiya.

source_anchors.notes: Stokes's theorem turns the punctured-zone boundary integral into the vanishing sum over node charges.

representation: Global Stokes-theorem step on the punctured Brillouin zone.

math_expressions.notes: Charge cancellation from a closed form and the boundary of the punctured Brillouin zone.

step_justification: The punctured Brillouin zone has boundary equal to the union of small spheres around the bad points.
The pulled-back area form is closed away from the bad points.
Stokes's theorem converts the bulk integral of an exact form to the boundary sum of local charges.
retrieval_hints: Use when a proof must show the precise Stokes step behind the lattice charge-cancellation theorem.
